NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: all 33 functional commands read the primary input from stdin by default; 31 write the primary result to stdout; every command can append a JSONL audit record
Gotree_0.5.2: Native: commands accept stdin and emit stdout
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Partial: most tree transformations require input file paths and may create derived files; selected reports use stdout
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Native: programs accept stdin and emit stdout
decision_rule: Native requires a documented tree-producing command to accept stdin and emit its primary result to stdout without a temporary file

task: Explicit Newick interpretation and ambiguity rejection
NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: selectable ETE format, automatic detection, strict automatic mode, and quoted-name control
Gotree_0.5.2: Not identified: Newick is accepted, but no documented equivalent of parser dialect selection or strict ambiguous-numeric-label rejection
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Not identified in reviewed command help
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Not identified in the v1.6 manual
decision_rule: Count only controls that distinguish alternative interpretations of syntactically readable Newick, not generic parse errors

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: one report covers parse status, duplicate or empty tips, branch-length and support problems, rooting, ultrametricity, binarity, and cross-tree consistency
Gotree_0.5.2: Partial: parsing, rootedness statistics, monophyly, and tip-set comparison are separate operations
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Partial: task-specific checks and statistics are available, but no equivalent consolidated preflight command was identified
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Partial: parsing and several consistency checks are distributed across utilities
decision_rule: Native requires one user-facing operation that reports representation, structural, and collection-level checks

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: prune, rename, collapse, remove or fill annotations, rescale, resolve polytomies, remove singletons, and join tabular properties to tips or clades
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Native: pruning, renaming, branch collapse, branch-length multiplication, and related operations are documented
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Native: pruning, renaming, rerooting, topology editing, support handling, and related filters are documented
decision_rule: Grouped row records established overlap and is not used as a novelty claim

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: outgroup, transferred root, midpoint, minimal ancestor deviation, and taxonomy-based rooting
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Partial: user-specified outgroup rooting; no documented midpoint, MAD, or taxonomy-based mode was identified
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Partial: explicit outgroup and longest-edge fallback
decision_rule: Modes count only when exposed in the reviewed command-line interface

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: diff reports clade-level compatibility; compose and transfer map roots, names, support, lengths, and arbitrary NHX properties, with conservative projection onto partially overlapping tip sets
Gotree_0.5.2: Partial: annotation from a comparison tree and node-name/comment transfer are supported
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Native: internal annotations are matched by descendant-taxon bipartitions
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Not identified in the v1.6 manual
decision_rule: Simple tip renaming or positional copying does not qualify

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: clade frequencies, weighted strict/majority/greedy consensus, optional reference tree, and branch-length summaries
Gotree_0.5.2: Native: consensus plus classical and transfer bootstrap support
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Native: strict or majority consensus and several gene-tree discordance analyses
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Partial: support calculations are available; no consensus-tree operation was identified
decision_rule: Native requires at least consensus or branch-frequency/support calculation across a tree collection

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: NCBI, NCBI+APG IV, or user backbones can produce constraints; NCBI/OpenTree/TimeTree can inform rooting
Gotree_0.5.2: Partial: NCBI taxonomy can be downloaded, pruned, and used to annotate trees, but no integrated species-label-aware constraint workflow was identified
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Not identified in reviewed command help
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Not identified in the v1.6 manual
decision_rule: External taxonomy must directly determine a returned topology or root

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: tip tables can be attached and aggregated onto clades; grouped monophyly tests, maximum-PD or ranked representative sampling, and clade skimming use trait filters
Gotree_0.5.2: Partial: monophyly of a supplied tip set; tree sampling is over input trees rather than tips
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Partial: monophyly tests and phylogenetic-diversity calculations; no equivalent trait-filtered representative-tip selection was identified
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Not identified in the v1.6 manual
decision_rule: Selection must choose tips using tree structure plus supplied metadata; monophyly-only implementations are partial

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: Mk likelihood under ER/SYM/ARD, marginal probabilities, maximum-a-posteriori states, and stochastic maps
Gotree_0.5.2: Partial: most-parsimonious ancestral characters and sequences
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Native: discrete model fitting, stochastic character mapping, and parsimony character mapping are documented
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Not identified in the v1.6 manual
decision_rule: Likelihood reconstruction, stochastic mapping, and parsimony are recorded separately in the detailed audit; this grouped row denotes any native categorical-ASR workflow

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: user calibrations or TimeTree point/interval estimates can be converted to MCMCtree-labelled Newick
decision_rule: Chronogram plotting or general node annotation does not qualify

NWKIT_0.28.0: Native: rectangular/radial tree drawing with support and categorical-trait annotation; species-image retrieval is a separate command
Gotree_0.5.2: Native: text, PNG, SVG, and Cytoscape outputs
PhyKIT_2.3.0: Native: tree, chronogram, trait-map, network, and other plots
Newick_Utilities_1.6: Native: text, PostScript, and SVG tree displays
decision_rule: Grouped row records overlap and is not used as a novelty claim
